channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ex-finance minster Chrystia Freeland is running to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Chrystia Freeland, whose abrupt resignation as finance minister last month forced Justin Trudeau's exit as prime minister, said she is running to be the next leader of the Liberal Party and Prime Minister of Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of dead sea turtles are washing ashore on India's coast
item.description: Over 400 endangered sea turtles have washed ashore on India’s east coast near the city of Chennai in the last two weeks in an event not witnessed in over two decades.
item.dc:creator: Sibi Arasu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia, Iran to seal partnership treaty days before Trump takes office
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian will meet in Moscow to sign a partnership pact as the two nations brace for President-elect Trump's return.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli security cabinet recommends approving ceasefire deal
item.description: Israel’s security cabinet has recommended approving a deal that would pause the fighting in Gaza and release dozens of hostages held by militants. The deal will now go to the full cabinet for approval before the ceasefire goes into effect.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French president in Lebanon to meet the country's new leaders and discuss ceasefire with Israel
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — France’s president began a visit to Lebanon Friday, where he will meet the crisis-hit country’s newly elected leaders, as the nation attempts to recover from the 14-month Israel-Hezbollah war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South African miner describes horrors for those who spent months underground
item.description: A miner who spent six months in a disused mine in South Africa has spoken of the horror he experienced after a police operation cut off food and basic supplies to thousands who were illegally digging for gold.
item.dc:creator: Mogomotsi Magome, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia and Iran have a troubled history despite their current alliance
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin hosted his Iranian counterpart Friday for the signing of a broad pact between Moscow and Tehran.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Lithuania vows to boost defense spending to 5-6% of GDP, citing the threat of Russian aggression
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Lithuania has decided to raise its spending on defense to between 5% and 6% of overall national economic output starting in 2026 due to the threat of Russian aggression in the region, Lithuanian President Gitanas Nausėda said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lithuania vows to boost defense spending to 5-6% of GDP, citing the threat of Russian aggression
item.description: Lithuania's president says his country has made the decision to raise its spending on defense to between 5% and 6% of overall national economic output starting in 2026.
item.dc:creator: By Liudas Dapkus, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel's security Cabinet recommends approval of ceasefire in Gaza; deal now goes to full Cabinet
item.description: Israel’s security Cabinet has recommended approval of a ceasefire deal after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed an agreement had been reached that would pause the 15-month war with Hamas in Gaza and release dozens of hostages held by militants there.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia and Iran sign a partnership treaty to deepen their ties in the face of Western sanctions
item.description: The presidents of Russia and Iran have signed a broad cooperation pact to deepen their partnership amid stinging Western sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Vladimir Isachenkov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China's population falls for a third straight year, posing challenges for its government and economy
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China's population fell last year for the third straight year, its government said Friday, pointing to further demographic challenges for the world's second most populous nation, which is now facing both an aging population and an emerging shortage of working age people.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani court sentences ex-PM Imran Khan and his wife to 14 and 7 years in prison in graft case
item.description: A defense lawyer says a Pakistani court has sentenced the country’s already-imprisoned former Prime Minister Imran Khan and his wife to 14 and seven years in jail after finding them guilty of corruption.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Xi's special representative Han Zheng to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: China’s vice president will attend President-elect Trump’s inauguration, according to a Thursday statement from the Foreign Ministry Spokesperson. “At U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Former Bank of England governor Mark Carney enters race to be Canada’s next prime minister
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Mark Carney, the first non-Brit to run the Bank of England since it was founded in 1694 and the former head of Canada’s central bank, said Thursday he is entering the race to be Canada’s next prime minister following the resignation of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK leader Starmer signs ‘100-year partnership’ agreement with Ukraine during trip to Kyiv
item.description: As part of a European show of support, U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer has signed a 100-year partnership agreement with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Danish prime minister tells Trump Greenland will decide its future
item.description: The Danish prime minister told President-elect Trump that Greenland will decide its future in a call Wednesday, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: UK leader Starmer signs '100-year partnership' agreement with Ukraine during trip to Kyiv
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer signed a 100-year partnership agreement with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Thursday, part of a European show of support and promises to keep helping Ukraine endure in its nearly three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK backs local inquiries into child sexual abuse gangs after Musk stoked controversy
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's government said Thursday that it will support a series of local inquiries into cases of organized child sexual abuse in the wake of a furor largely stoked by the world's richest man Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American college rape suspect is being extradited to the US
item.description: Ian Cleary, who is accused of sexually assaulting a Gettysburg College student at a party in 2013, is being sent back to the U.S., officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis injured as Vatican confirms 2nd fall in matter of weeks
item.description: Pope Francis fell for the second time in six weeks on Thursday, injuring his right arm and requiring a sling, the Vatican says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: O'Leary on Gaza agreement: Adversaries 'scared poo-poo' of Trump
item.description: Entrepreneur and “Shark Tank” investor Kevin O’Leary said Wednesday that the ceasefire-hostage agreement between Israel and Hamas was reached Wednesday because foreign adversaries are scared of President-elect Trump, who returns to the Oval Office next week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Leaders from rival Kurdish groups look to mend ties during Syria upheaval
item.description: IRBIL, Iraq (AP) — The leaders of two previously rival Kurdish groups met in northern Iraq on Thursday in an apparent step toward reconciliation at a time when the political upheaval in Syria has left Kurds in the region facing an uncertain future.
item.dc:creator: STELLA MARTANY and QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu says 'last-minute crisis' with Hamas holding up approval of Gaza truce and hostage deal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said Thursday his Cabinet won’t meet to approve the agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages until Hamas backs down, accusing the group of reneging on parts of the agreement in an atte
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Two Americans to be released from Gaza in first phase of ceasefire deal
item.description: Two Americans, Keith Siegel and Sagui Dekel-Chen, are expected to be released as part of the first phase of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal that emerged Wednesday after being held for 15-months by Hamas since the start of the war in Gaza, according to a senior administration official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu delays Gaza cease-fire vote, accusing Hamas of trying to back out of deal
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday accused Hamas of backing out of a cease-fire deal to release hostages and bring a pause to more than a year of fighting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Puerto Rico asks Trump for help after Venezuela's Maduro threatens invasion
item.description: Puerto Rican voters have overwhelmingly chosen to keep their U.S. citizenship, granted in 1917 by an act of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Details need to be addressed in Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: When a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is finalized depends on the handling of specific implementation details
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Impeached South Korean president detained weeks after martial law chaos
item.description: South Korean law enforcement detained impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol to bring him in for questioning following his short-lived martial law declaration last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas OKs draft agreement of a Gaza ceasefire and the release of some hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: 84-year-old man fights off robber using pair of jeans
item.description: YORKSHIRE, U.K. (WFLA) — An 84-year-old man used a pair of jeans to fight off a would-be robber as he did his laundry in South Yorkshire.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Polish town invites Musk to buy castle for his European HQ
item.description: Tech billionaire is reportedly looking for a sizeable location, possibly a castle in Italy, as a hub for his operations in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korean soldiers in Russia resort to suicide amid capture of first POWs by Ukraine
item.description: North Korean soldiers fighting in Russia's Kursk region against Ukraine have begun resorting to drastic measures to avoid capture, including "finishing off" fellow soldiers and suicide by grenade.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Canada deploying 60 firefighters to help with California blazes
item.description: In a post on X, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada was helping aid the Los Angeles area as several wildfires continue devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Family of American hostage in Gaza remains hopeful their son, 21, will return
item.description: Edan Alexander, an American hostage, celebrated his second birthday in captivity after Hamas kidnapped him more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation
